categories.name: Leadership & Decision-Making
categories.definition: Focuses on leadership thinking, strategic direction, decision frameworks, and how individuals or organizations choose paths forward under uncertainty. Includes executive thinking, long-range planning, and the psychology of leading complex initiatives.

categories.name: Innovation & Systems Thinking
categories.definition: Explores new ways of thinking, reframing challenges, systems design, and paradigm-shifting approaches that change how problems and opportunities are understood. Includes cross-disciplinary thinking, structural redesign, and new models.

categories.name: Mearth Framework (Earth-Moon Construct)
categories.definition: Covers the Earth-Moon ecosystem framework and its economic, societal, technological, and philosophical implications. Includes discussions of the Mearth construct and long-term interconnected system being built.

categories.name: Economy, Finance & Markets
categories.definition: Addresses economic systems, capital flows, market structures, investment models, financial innovation, and long-term value creation.

categories.name: Technology & Engineering
categories.definition: Focuses on engineering, technical systems, applied science, and the development or deployment of technologies.

categories.name: Robotics & Automation
categories.definition: Covers robotics systems, automation technologies, autonomous operations, and machine-assisted processes.

categories.name: Energy & Power Systems
categories.definition: Addresses generation, storage, transmission, and management of energy and power systems.

categories.name: Materials, Mining & Supply Chains
categories.definition: Covers materials science, mining systems, resource extraction, processing, logistics, and supply chain structures.

categories.name: Food, Agriculture & Bio-Systems
categories.definition: Focuses on food production, agricultural systems, biological cycles, and sustainable resource cultivation.

categories.name: Water, Environment & Resilience
categories.definition: Addresses water systems, environmental conditions, ecological resilience, and long-term sustainability of natural resources.

categories.name: Education & Learning Systems
categories.definition: Covers education models, learning frameworks, curriculum design, and knowledge transfer systems.

categories.name: Governance, Law & Standards
categories.definition: Addresses governance models, regulatory systems, legal frameworks, standards creation, and institutional coordination.

categories.name: Human Behavior & Psychology
categories.definition: Explores human thinking, behavior, motivation, perception, and decision psychology.

categories.name: Infrastructure & Logistics
categories.definition: Covers physical and operational infrastructure including transportation, construction, supply movement, and large-scale systems deployment.
